title: Requirement Ledger
description: Δ-19. Durable, project-level requirement ledger (archive/2026-06-23-requirement-ledger-design.md). One REQ item per requirement, normalized from every intake channel (§3.1). Records ONLY Customer-authored disposition (an engine/agent may seed a NEW item at `pending` only; all decided values remain Customer-authored) + provenance + audit history. It does NOT store coverage (covers_req_ids lives on the signed campaign-plan milestone, §3.4) and does NOT store delivery_status (that is a DERIVED, read-only scope_report projection, §3.5 — never written here). Phase 2-alpha backbone; additive — absence is byte-identical to today.

requirements.description: The REQ items. May be empty (a freshly-initialized ledger).

id.description: Unique requirement id (REQ-prefixed, path-safe). Referenced by signed campaign-plan milestones' covers_req_ids.

statement.description: One human-readable requirement, end-user-observable where possible.

source.description: Provenance — which intake channel produced this REQ (§3.1).

ref.description: Optional pointer to the originating artifact (e.g. a proposal path, a failure-brief id).

customer_disposition.description: CUSTOMER AUTHORITY ONLY (§4.E, Constitution §1.3/§1.7) — agents propose, only the Customer sets a DECIDED value. NEVER written by any engine/agent, EXCEPT an engine/agent MAY seed `pending` (the undecided initialization sentinel) on a NEW item; it MUST NEVER write or change a decided value (accepted|deferred|skipped|dropped|modified) — every transition out of `pending` stays Customer authority. For an UNSIGNED-backlog REQ the Customer edits this directly (§3.3); for a REQ bound to SIGNED scope this field is DISPLAY-ONLY and an actual change goes through re-sign / research_contract_revision (the ledger reflects, never drives, §3.3).

gap_type.description: For gap-derived REQs (matured bad-case / acceptance gap): unmet_existing = an existing REQ not yet met (default); new_scope = genuinely new (Customer/Research-confirmed). §3.1.

surface.description: OW-M3 input contract: does meeting this requirement produce something the end user OPERATES (browser-operable UI / a user journey)? user_facing ⇒ any milestone whose covers_req_ids include this REQ MUST be accepted via browser-E2E (M3) — no downgrade to static. Agent-proposed, CUSTOMER-CONFIRMED by being signed into scope; reclassification of a signed REQ ⇒ re-sign (Customer authority). Distinct from customer_disposition. OPTIONAL v1 (absence ⇒ the mandate is dormant for milestones covering it, and the OW-M3 sign-off gate refuses a plan that covers an unclassified REQ).

surface_status.description: OW-AUTO advisory authoring signal. 'proposed' = agent-proposed surface not yet human-confirmed; 'confirmed' = a human accepted it at authoring time. ADVISORY ONLY — the engine never gates on this and it is NOT bound into the signed scope hash (nor the requirement-context sidecar / acceptance_input_hash — it is projected out before the resolver hash / gap-report facts); the binding confirmation is the Customer signing the covering plan. Absent ⇒ treated as 'proposed'.

surface_confidence.description: OW-AUTO advisory: the agent's self-assessed confidence in its proposed surface. The wizard/loop escalates only 'low' items for a lightweight human confirm before sign-off. ADVISORY ONLY — not bound into the signed hash or acceptance_input_hash, never gated on. Absent ⇒ treated as 'high' (flows without escalation).

relates_to_req_ids.description: For gap_type=unmet_existing: the existing REQ(s) this gap is an instance of.

elaboration.description: ONE-WAY references INTO research artifacts (e.g. 'brief-007#clause-2'). The link lives here, not in the brief (research-brief.schema.json stays additionalProperties:false).

supersedes.description: When customer_disposition=modified replaces an older REQ: the id this one supersedes (the old REQ flips to 'modified', the new one starts 'pending'). §3.3.

history.description: Append-only audit of disposition/provenance changes. Every customer_disposition change appends one entry.

at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the change (injected clock in tests).
